title: The 1% Rule of Improvement
author: From: Atomic Habits
content: Improving by 1% a day doesn't look like much, but over a year, it results in a 37x improvement. Consistency is the compound interest of self-growth.

title: Decision Fatigue
author: From: Thinking, Fast and Slow
content: The more decisions you make in a day, the lower the quality of your decisions becomes. Automate your morning; preserve your willpower for strategic moves.

title: Commitment Bias
author: From: Influence
content: We have an inner pressure to be consistent with what we have already done. This makes us hold on to bad investments too long.

title: The Power of Inaction
author: From: The Art of Thinking Clearly
content: In a fast-moving market, 'doing something' is often just a reaction to anxiety. Strategic patience is a rare and profitable skill.

title: Personal Strategy as a System
author: From: Principles
content: Treat your life and your portfolio as a system. When something goes wrong, don't fix the incident—fix the system that allowed the incident.
action: Fix the system, not the incident.
